subject: Update realtek drivers from upstream (#44)
author: gkrzystek

impact.effect_if_absent: No impact to 6.18 build. In-kernel Realtek drivers are not used; they are disabled in CONFIG. Coverage is split: RTL8812AU and RTL8811AU/RTL8821AU are replaced by external BR2 packages sourced from morrownr (package/rtl8812au, package/rtl8821au-morrownr); RTL8811CU/RTL8821CU and RTL8812BU/RTL8822BU are instead replaced by MAINLINE in-kernel drivers (CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m, CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m) under the defconfig's 'mainline-first' policy (mister_de10nano_defconfig P3.1/v9 comment block) -- the morrownr rtl8821cu-morrownr/rtl88x2bu packages this commit's diff touches are sourced (Config.in present) but explicitly disabled (# ... is not set) to avoid a USB-ID bind conflict with the mainline drivers.

userspace_coupling.interface: cfg80211/nl80211 (unchanged across this commit and in BR2 packages)

superseded_by: package/rtl8812au (morrownr/8812au-20210820 @ 8cac6f43316a56cc89cc8cb532cd6c6ae14c4805, merge titled 'support from kernels 6.17-7.0') -- ACTIVE: BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y (mister_de10nano_defconfig:565). RTL8812AU has no mainline USB driver.

package/rtl8821au-morrownr (morrownr/8821au-20210708 @ 3a7cdb591b64d99d2670e455bde67c8ab338525b, merge titled 'kernel-7.0-build-fixes') -- ACTIVE: BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR=y (mister_de10nano_defconfig:567). RTL8811AU/RTL8821AU has no mainline USB driver.

package/rtl8821cu-morrownr (morrownr/8821cu-20210916 @ 7f63a9da2e8ed83403f6f920e9b1628a37b38ef4, merge titled 'fix/linux-6.18') -- pin verified but INACTIVE: # BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821CU_MORROWNR is not set (mister_de10nano_defconfig:570). RTL8811CU/RTL8821CU is actually covered by the MAINLINE in-tree driver: CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m (drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8821cu.c in /mnt/source/linux; confirmed =m at output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config:1712 and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:491).

package/rtl88x2bu (morrownr/88x2bu-20210702 @ fecac340fb117eb979f4bb6d28e29730384c382b, merge titled 'fix-kernel-6.18-compatibility') -- pin verified but INACTIVE: # BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU is not set (mister_de10nano_defconfig:580). RTL8812BU/RTL8822BU is actually covered by the MAINLINE in-tree driver: CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m (drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8822bu.c; confirmed =m at output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config:1705 and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:484). Switched deliberately per mister_de10nano_defconfig:571-579: mainline's mac80211 path gets WPA3/SAE/PMF right where out-of-tree 88x2bu advertised SAE+CMAC but failed WPA3-only association (status_code=1, hardware-verified per that comment).

notes:
In-kernel Realtek drivers in MiSTer's 5.15 Linux fork (rtl8812au, rtl8821au, rtl8821cu, rtl88x2bu) are NOT used in our 6.18 build — they are completely superseded, but NOT uniformly by external BR2 packages (see CORRECTION below). The in-kernel versions are not enabled in our kernel CONFIG (no CONFIG_RTL8812AU/8821AU/8821CU/8822BU entries in linux.config), and the Kconfig options for these drivers do not exist in vanilla 6.18. Commit 993b82e (Mar 2023) updated these drivers in the stock MiSTer 5.15 fork with two types of changes: (1) debug output reductions (CONFIG_RTW_DEBUG=y→n, CONFIG_PROC_DEBUG=y→n in Makefiles) and (2) cosmetic cleanups (whitespace, trailing lines). Userspace coupling via cfg80211/nl80211 is preserved across the transition regardless of which of the two replacement paths (out-of-tree package vs. mainline rtw88) ends up binding a given chip.

CORRECTION (sonnet-verify): The original 'superseded_by' list named all four morrownr packages (rtl8812au, rtl8821au-morrownr, rtl8821cu-morrownr, rtl88x2bu) as THE replacement, and impact.effect_if_absent claimed uniform BR2-package coverage. Checking the live mister_de10nano_defconfig (not just the package/ directory, which only proves the packages are *sourced*, not *enabled*) shows this is half true: BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU=y and BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR=y are active, but BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821CU_MORROWNR and BR2_PACKAGE_RTL88X2BU are both explicitly `# ... is not set` (defconfig:570,580). Those two chip families (RTL8811CU/8821CU and RTL8812BU/8822BU) are instead covered by MAINLINE in-kernel drivers under a documented 'mainline-first' policy (defconfig:543-580, P3.1/v9): CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m and CONFIG_RTW88_8822BU=m, both confirmed set in output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config and board/mister/de10nano/linux.config. The defconfig's own comment explains WHY for the 8822BU case specifically: the out-of-tree 88x2bu advertised WPA3/SAE support but failed WPA3-only association on real hardware (status_code=1), while mainline rtw88's mac80211 path works correctly. This same staleness exists in docs/patch-provenance.md line 389, which also lists all six re-sourced package names (including rtl8821cu, rtl88x2bu) without the mainline-first caveat -- both that doc and this record predate the P3.1/v9 defconfig policy shift. Device_ids also amended: the original list (0x8812/0x881a/0x881b/0x881c/0x0811/0x0821/0x0823) covers only the RTL8812/RTL8821 'Default ID' entries shared across the fork's rtl8821cu and rtl88x2bu usb_intf.c ID tables; none of those IDs are actually RTL8822B-specific. Added 0xb812/0xb82c/0xb81a (RTL8822B 'Default ID' entries, confirmed in the fork's rtl88x2bu/os_dep/linux/usb_intf.c under #ifdef CONFIG_RTL8822B) so affected_hardware's claimed RTL8822BU coverage has a matching device ID.

Net effect on disposition: unchanged (dropped-deliberate is still correct -- this commit's diff does not survive to our tree either way) and severity/failure_mode unchanged (none/silent -- the chips are covered either way, just by a different subsystem than originally recorded). The pin verification requested by this audit task passed cleanly: all four package .mk files were checked directly (VERSION hash, SITE morrownr repo/tag, and the commit-message rationale strings) and match verbatim what's quoted in the original superseded_by list.

sonnet-verified: PASS with substantive correction (superseded_by/impact/device_ids amended; two of the four claimed morrownr packages are actually inactive, real coverage for those two chip families is mainline in-kernel rtw88, not BR2 out-of-tree packages).
